column1: # DOLCHEO+ encoding TSV — schema per canon/governance/writing-conventions.md
# D, O, L, C rows (6 cols): type
# Tab-separated. Order within file: D/O/L/C/H first, then O-open at end.
# Session: 2026-05-29 preview environment (isolated [env.preview] worker; DO blocks version preview URLs)

column2: Isolated preview environment as a separate [env.preview] worker
column3: Branch/staging work deploys to a separate worker transcode-mcp-preview via wrangler deploy --env preview, never touching production (transcode-mcp). Bindings are non-inheritable in Wrangler environments so each is redefined per-env against its own preview resources: a SEPARATE R2 bucket (transcode-mcp-audio-preview), the env's own AudioContainer DO namespace (distinct because the worker name differs) + container, and the account-level IMAGES binding. Production deploys stay wrangler deploy from a reviewed state. Recorded in canon/planning/2026-05-29-preview-environment.md; runbook in docs/CI.md.
column4: Cloudflare docs verified 2026-05-29: bindings/vars are non-inheritable and must be specified per environment; DO bindings are not inherited and resolve to distinct storage when the worker name differs. wrangler.toml parsed: prod bucket transcode-mcp-audio vs preview transcode-mcp-audio-preview (distinct). typecheck clean, 86 tests pass.
column5: wrangler versions upload with no new env (no preview URL for a DO worker — does not deliver the isolated preview); share prod R2 bucket (rejected by requirement and on merit — shared state defeats isolation); Workers Builds preview branches (still bound by the DO limitation; would have to target a separate env anyway — can layer on later).
column6: working — needs a one-time `wrangler r2 bucket create transcode-mcp-audio-preview` then first `wrangler deploy --env preview`

column2: Cloudflare does not mint version preview URLs for Workers that implement a Durable Object
column3: wrangler versions upload returns no preview URL for any Worker that implements a Durable Object, and uploading a version with DO migrations is unsupported (use wrangler deploy). Our worker implements AudioContainer, so the lightweight version-preview-URL path is unavailable; an isolated, clickable preview requires a separate named environment (its own worker name = its own workers.dev URL). Cloudflare describes the preview-URL gap as a temporary limitation.
column4: Cloudflare Workers docs (Preview URLs; Versions & Deployments), read 2026-05-29.
column6: working — revisit if Cloudflare removes the DO/preview-URL limitation

column2: Apply 90-day lifecycle GC to the preview R2 bucket; consider Workers Builds for per-PR previews
column3: After creating transcode-mcp-audio-preview, give it the same 90-day lifecycle rule as production so preview transcodes do not accumulate indefinitely. Optional later: connect Cloudflare Workers Builds so non-prod branches auto-deploy to --env preview and main deploys to production, automating the per-PR preview.

column4: Stop deploying branches to production; gate prod deploys to a reviewed state
column5: Historically branch iterations were shipped with wrangler deploy (production). Going forward, production deploys should come only from a reviewed state (e.g. main), with branch work on --env preview or wrangler dev --remote. Closes by documenting/[optionally] enforcing the rule in CI (deploy job runs only on main).
